name: HIVE Digital Technologies Ltd.

profile.description: HIVE Digital Technologies is the first publicly listed company to mine digital assets exclusively with green energy, operating next-generation blockchain and AI data centers across Canada, Sweden, and Paraguay. The company's 'dual engine' infrastructure serves both Bitcoin mining (25 EH/s capacity) and AI/high-performance computing (BUZZ HPC platform targeting 11,000 GPUs by end of 2026). Total renewable infrastructure footprint reaches 540 MW across three continents.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: HIVE executed a transformative Paraguay expansion in H1 FY2026, scaling global hashrate from 6 to 25 EH/s in six months — a 317% increase that CEO Aydin Kilic called the blueprint for all future growth [1]. BUZZ HPC achieved the #1 global ranking for AI cloud network download speed and is targeting $120M in annual run-rate revenue at 80% operating margin from 11,000 GPUs by end of 2026 [1]. The company's green-first architecture (17.5 J/TH fleet efficiency) and dual Bitcoin/AI infrastructure model represent genuine industry-defining innovation in clean compute.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Executive Chairman Frank Holmes has articulated a clear mission around clean, renewable computing — 'converting renewable energy into high-value digital infrastructure rapidly and responsibly' — backed by genuine renewable energy deployments across three continents [1]. The company's culture prioritizes efficiency-first growth over speculative expansion, and shareholder approvals at the March 5, 2026 annual meeting showed broad stakeholder alignment [1]. However, the company has no formal community investment program comparable to traditional CSR efforts, limiting the inspiration score.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: HIVE delivered record Q2 FY2026 revenue of $87.3M (up 285% YoY), record Q1 FY2026 revenue of $45.6M, and Adjusted EBITDA of $44.6M with net income of $35M in Q1 [1]. The company targets $750M ARR by end of 2026 and $100M revenue from HPC/AI alone [1]. However, crypto-correlated revenue creates high earnings volatility, cash declined to $22.6M in Q2, and the business model depends heavily on Bitcoin prices — making infrastructure scoring moderate despite strong recent growth.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: HIVE is uniquely positioned as an AI beneficiary rather than a victim of AI disruption — its BUZZ HPC platform directly serves the AI compute market, and the company is pivoting toward AI data centers as a core business segment [1]. Its GPU cloud business (BUZZ) is ranked #1 globally for AI cloud network download speed, demonstrating operational AI infrastructure competency [1]. The primary risk is that hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Amazon) build out sufficient proprietary GPU capacity to reduce demand for third-party AI cloud providers.

infinite_game.summary: HIVE's multi-year renewable energy infrastructure buildout, disciplined efficiency-first culture, and deliberate pivot from pure Bitcoin mining to a dual Bitcoin/AI infrastructure model demonstrate long-game thinking. CEO Kilic's 'flywheel' framing — efficiency leading to low costs leading to rapid deployment leading to more capacity — reflects compounding rather than speculative thinking.

just_cause.analysis: HIVE's mission of providing 'clean compute' powered exclusively by renewable energy is an industry-differentiating purpose with genuine environmental backing — all three continents of operations use renewable hydroelectric power [1].

trusting_teams.analysis: The consistent leadership team (Frank Holmes, Aydin Kilic, Susan McGee, Marcus New, Dave Perrill) and successful execution of the Paraguay expansion in six months demonstrate high team capability [1]. All resolutions passed at the March 2026 AGM.

worthy_rivals.analysis: HIVE benchmarks against Marathon Digital, Riot Platforms, and CleanSpark in Bitcoin mining, and against Coreweave and Lambda Labs in AI compute — but rarely references rivals in public communications.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The pivot from pure Bitcoin miner to dual Bitcoin/AI infrastructure company represents exactly the existential flexibility required to survive in the volatile crypto sector. The Paraguay buildout model — rapid, renewable, low-cost — is now the template for all future expansion [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Building a $100M+ AI revenue business from scratch while operating as a publicly listed Bitcoin miner — with associated reputation and volatility concerns — required genuine conviction in the dual-engine thesis [1].

investment_thesis: HIVE Digital Technologies is executing one of the most compelling dual-engine transformations in digital infrastructure: Q2 FY2026 revenue surged 285% YoY to $87.3M driven by the Paraguay expansion scaling hashrate from 6 to 25 EH/s, while BUZZ HPC ranks #1 globally for AI cloud download speed and targets $100M+ in AI/HPC revenue by end of 2026 [1]. The company's renewable energy moat (540 MW across three continents) provides a genuine cost and ESG advantage over coal-powered competitors, and Bitcoin halving dynamics favor efficient low-cost miners like HIVE as a long-term structural winner [1].
thesis_continued: HIVE's Bitcoin mining revenue is highly correlated to BTC price — a 30% decline in BTC would materially impair revenue and could trigger negative EBITDA given the leverage in the model. The path from $87M quarterly revenue to $750M ARR is extremely ambitious and requires both Bitcoin price stability and successful BUZZ HPC ramp to 11,000 GPUs by December 2026 [1]. Cash position declined to $22.6M in Q2 (vs. $71.9M in Q1), reflecting heavy CapEx investment in Paraguay. Hyperscaler GPU buildout could commoditize AI cloud services and compress BUZZ margins before the business reaches scale.
key_catalysts: BUZZ HPC reaching 11,000 GPU target by end of 2026 — at $120M ARR and 80% operating margins, this transforms HIVE's earnings profile significantly [1]
Bitcoin price sustaining above $80,000 — HIVE's 25 EH/s capacity at 17.5 J/TH creates massive operating leverage to BTC price appreciation
Liquid-cooled Canada and Sweden AI data centers coming online in H2 2026 — adds second and third AI compute campuses beyond Toronto [1]
key_risks: Bitcoin price decline below $60,000 — crypto revenue accounts for ~94% of current revenue; $10K BTC price move ~$13M/quarter impact on mining profitability
Hyperscaler GPU supply expansion reducing BUZZ HPC pricing power — AWS, Google, and Microsoft are all adding GPU capacity that could commoditize H100/Blackwell rental rates
Execution risk on 11,000 GPU target — Blackwell GPU supply constraints or data center construction delays could miss $100M AI revenue target for 2026 [1]
sources: HIVE Digital Technologies Q1 and Q2 FY2026 Earnings Results and CEO Commentary, HIVE Corporate Newsroom / Yahoo Finance, 2025-2026
